title: Eva The hyperactive imagination of a depressed boy
display_title: Eva The hyperactive imagination of a depressed boy
wikitext:
[]: Eva: The hyperactive imagination of a depressed boy             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Fri Apr 23, 2010 7:41 pm This is my first post here, so hi. Any way, while I don't subscribe to this theory myself, I came up with it, unless someone else did and I didn't know. EoTV and EoE take place in this order: episode 25, EoE, episode 26. Shinji is just a boy suffering from depression. The alternate universe high school is the real world. He begins to go through depression, so he creates an alternate reality in his own mind. This is episode 1-7. As he begins to get better, his imaginary world goes into a typical giant robot anime, with some of his own twists. This makes up 8-~16. As his depression worsens, he begins to torture the alternate versions of his friends: ~16-23. Eventually his depression gets to the point where he tortures himself more and more often, represented by Kaworu in episode 24. Episode 25 is him considering suicide. He decides against it, and goes back to his fantasy world. The story in his fantasy world ends with his death by reversion to LCL. The rest of EoE is symbolic. The Pre-Third Impact Instrumentality is him considering suicide, deciding to, attempting it, and failing, although going into a coma. In his coma, he's cut off from the rest of the world, represented by everyone dissolving into LCL. Than comes 26. While in his coma, he probes his own mind, and eventually becomes happy with who he is. After this, he wakes up, with Asuka caressing his face, causing him to break down in tears, and return to reality.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Fri Apr 23, 2010 8:35 pm sounds an awful lot like this

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Apr 23, 2010 8:57 pm Merridian : I was thinking more of this , myself. TehDonutKing : While it's fine to think of EoTV (Episodes 25 and 26) being concurrent with EoE (Episodes 25' and 26' ), thinking Episode 26 comes after EoE doesn't really jive for a few reasons. (The similar imagery of expressions and a blue sky in the Episode 26' group photo compared to the 26 "Congratulations!" scene, the 26 theater being blown away in a similar manner to the EoE Black Moon being destroyed, Shinji's similar lines of that discussing "Being/staying here" at the end of 26 and his final speech in EoE, "Goodbye mother" mentioned as on-screen text or dialogue in both endings...) All that said, what happens throughout the events of the series is grounded in reality that Shinji and the cast know and are a part of. Both the final 2 series episodes and EoE, however, have Instrumentality (and Third Impact in EoE) occur. Whether or not Shinji rejects Instrumentality and returns to reality in both ending(s) depends on your interpretation, though most of the folk around here (myself included) feel that Shinji did reject Instrumentality in both.

[]:             Quote Post by Merridian » Fri Apr 23, 2010 9:18 pm ^I was mostly referring to Sheldon's Shinji-1 concept . Not an interpretation I can agree with, but it was pretty interesting stuff anyway

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Fri Apr 23, 2010 9:28 pm My first thought was that EoTV was straight forward, and he accepts Instrumentality. I'm still trying to work out how it fits with EoE, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Apr 23, 2010 9:36 pm This is an archived thread, but it explains things pretty nicely. And still old, but classic and relevant: http://www.angelfire.com/anime4/mdwigs/endings.html In terms of actual discussion: Very very old thread started by yours truly when I first arrived: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=1833 (I hate my old posts, Baww! They're made of such fail with my rambling! ) Most recent thread (Bump this one if you want to discuss things) regarding EoTV vs EoE: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=8122 What IS Concurrency? (Just what does it mean for EoTV and EoE to fall into such, or not). http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=8141 Hope that helps! ;)

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Apr 23, 2010 10:27 pm ^ Yes, has the same scent of fail about it.

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Fri Apr 23, 2010 11:06 pm Just because the story represents his psyche doesn't mean he's imagining it. It's deliberately paced to represent things going on in his head, but it's not necessarily not happening.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Fri Apr 23, 2010 11:16 pm I actually disagree with my own theory on this and argue weak concurrency, but it is a possibility. I've had quite a few parallels to this theory in my own life, which is what made me consider this.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Apr 23, 2010 11:21 pm For all the links to other NGE threads this theory reminds me most of that Pokemon essay that theorized that everything in the series took place in Ash's mind or something like that.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Fri Apr 23, 2010 11:23 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: For all the links to other NGE threads this theory reminds me most of that Pokemon essay that theorized that everything in the series took place in Ash's mind or something like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Apr 23, 2010 11:45 pm I will grant there's certainly more in NGE that invites such an interpretation to be considered.

[]:             Quote Post by TehDonutKing » Sat Apr 24, 2010 12:08 am Yeah. I'll point it out when I find it. I'm watching Eva for the second time, so I'll probably notice more.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Apr 24, 2010 12:29 am NemZ wrote: ^ Yes, has the same scent of fail about it.

[]:             Quote Post by backseatjesus » Sat Apr 24, 2010 1:26 am Eva Yojimbo wrote: For all the links to other NGE threads this theory reminds me most of that Pokemon essay that theorized that everything in the series took place in Ash's mind or something like that.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Apr 24, 2010 2:30 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: Huh??

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Apr 24, 2010 3:03 am NemZ: Right, right. I just thought you were replying to something I wrote. Thanks for clarifying.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Apr 24, 2010 3:43 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: NemZ: Right, right. I just thought you were replying to something I wrote.

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Sat Apr 24, 2010 5:20 am I disagree with this interpretation. It's underanalyzing a fundamentally nuanced show. A structural cop-out content with meta-theorizing. No offense meant to the OP, however. I just don't agree with it principle.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Apr 24, 2010 11:59 am I'm getting flashbacks to a thread on ANF a few years back which started with a similar thesis.
